title: rubus drejeri subsp. hibernicus
intro: Rubus drejeri subsp. hibernicus, also known as the Irish Raspberry or Red Raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus drejeri plant, which is a type of bramble or wild raspberry. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health conditions. The health benefits of Rubus drejeri subsp. hibernicus include its ability to support digestive health, reduce menstrual cramps, and provide antioxidant protection against cell damage and aging. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and fruits are also used in herbal preparations, such as jams and syrups, which can be consumed for their medicinal and nutritional value.
